description: Building permits issued by City of Mesa. Included: All building permits applied. description: City of Mesa population figures come from the U.S. Census Bureau’s Population Estimates Program (PEP), released annually (as of July 1 each year). See Population and Housing Unit Estimates. Arizona uses these PEP estimates for state revenue sharing under A.R.S. § 42-5033.01. This dataset is the official population base for all per-capita city metrics (e.g., crimes or traffic collisions per 1,000 residents). 2025–2040 population projections are provided by the Maricopa County Association of Gover

description: ***In January 2020, as part of implementing a new citywide police incident reporting system the City began moving away from reporting crime to the FBI Unified Crime Reporting (UCR) Program and instead to the new National Incident-Based Reporting System (NIBRS) format. For this reason, 2020 UCR data may have inconsistencies and/or inaccuracies. A filtered view of UCR data is available for 2011-2019 at https://citydata.mesaaz.gov/Police/Crime-Reporting-Statistics-Uniform-Crime-Reporting/bfen-qa5d

description: Listing of all City expenses per department. This data includes transactions paid by check as well as payroll transactions and transactions paid by wire or electronic draft. Includes record date, payee, amount and the accounting string that describes which fund, organizational department and the activity roll up of the department that benefited from the transaction. The data contained on this website is for informational purposes only and represents expenditure transactions of the City of Mesa.

Valley Metro uses APC (Automatic People Counter) technology to generate the number of riders that they report on. Data reporting can be delayed

name: Traffic Counts

columns.name: Standard Count Year
columns.description: Each traffic count is planned to be taken in either an odd or even year. Valid values: ODD, EVEN.

columns.name: Traffic Direction
columns.description: The direction of the traffic volume.

columns.name: Direction
columns.description: The cardinal direction of the block number. Values: N, S, E, W. The E and W hundred blocks begin with 0 at the Center Street alignment. The N and S hundred blocks begin with 0 at the Main Street alignment.

columns.name: Calendar Year
columns.description: This is the date when the traffic count was taken in year YYYY

columns.name: location
columns.description: Latitude and Longitude of count

columns.name: Date of Count
columns.description: This is the date when the traffic count was taken.

columns.name: Bi-directional Volume
columns.description: This is the traffic volume over a one-day weekday period with both directions combined.

columns.name: Volume of Traffic
columns.description: This is the traffic volume over a one-day weekday period.

columns.name: Block
columns.description: The hundred block on the street in which the count is taken. A hundred block is typically 1/8 mile in length. The hundred block numbers west of Center Street increase the further west they are from Center St. The hundred block numbers east of Center Street increase the further east they are from Center St. The hundred block numbers south of Main Street increase the further south they are from Main St. The hundred block numbers north of Main Street increase the further north they are from Man St.

columns.name: Location ID
columns.description: The unique location code used to identify each count location. Sample values: 1-004-61-62, 1-072-07-08, etc.

columns.name: On Street
columns.description: The street on which this count is taken. Sample values: Alma School Road, Extension, 8th Avenue, etc.

description: The Average Weekday Traffic Volume is a count of the number of vehicle axle pairs (a car has 2 axles) passing through a given location on an average weekday. This is the traffic volume over a one-day weekday period and is a summary of the data that is collected in 15-minute intervals. The 15-minute interval traffic volumes for a particular location are available through Mesa’s Interactive Traffic Count Map application at https://gis.mesaaz.gov/TrafficCounts

name: Feeding Mesa - Revenues and Expenditures

columns.name: Fund Code
columns.description: The number assigned to the governmental segment that benefited from the transaction.

columns.name: Sub Fund Code
columns.description: The numbers assigned for the breakdown of a fund based on purpose, classification, and other specific characteristics.

columns.name: Sub Activity Name
columns.description: Activities are broken down into sub-activities for reporting and analysis purposes.

columns.name: Fiscal Year
columns.description: A 12-month period of time to which the Annual Budget applies and at the end of which, a governmental unit determines its financial position and the results of its operations. For the City of Mesa, the fiscal year is July 1 through June 30

columns.name: Revenue Source Code
columns.description: The number assigned to a revenue source for tracking purposes.

columns.name: Document Code
columns.description: The number assigned to the type of document the transaction was recorded in the accounting system.

columns.name: Document Department Code
columns.description: Coded documentation of the employees associated with the revenue or expenditure, by department.

columns.name: Unit Name
columns.description: The organizational unit within the department that benefited from the transaction. Examples include Planning, Development Services, Energy Resources, Electric Utility and Gas Utility.

columns.name: Fiscal Year Date
columns.description: Fiscal year of the revenue or expenditure

columns.name: Reporting Code
columns.description: The number assigned to a reporting mechanism used to track the expenditures related to a specific event or item. Examples are shows at the Mesa Arts Center and Park expenses.

columns.name: Sub Activity Code
columns.description: The number assigned to a sub-activity.

columns.name: Activity Code
columns.description: The number assigned to the activity the department was engaged in that led to the transaction.

columns.name: Month Valid
columns.description: Month derived from the “Date Valid” field.

columns.name: Unit Code
columns.description: A Chart of Accounts element that functions as both a budget/expense center and an organizational level element. In this case, it indicates the department that benefited from the transaction.

columns.name: Budget Year
columns.description: Year of the revenue or expenditure

columns.name: Department Code
columns.description: The number assigned to the department that benefited from the transaction.

columns.name: Budget Year Date
columns.description: Year of the revenue or expenditure

columns.name: Department Name
columns.description: The department that benefited from the transaction.

columns.name: Fund Name
columns.description: The governmental segment that benefited from the transaction. Examples include Cemetery, Transit and Arts and Culture.

columns.name: Activity Name
columns.description: The activity that the department was engaged in that led to the transaction. Activities are generally recurring functions such as accounting, inspections, or planning. Activities are used to assign accounting entries to a core business process.

columns.name: Object Code
columns.description: The number assigned to the type of expense being paid for a tangible item or service obtained. In the private sector, this would be called a general ledger account number, however in the government sector, these are most often referred to as object codes.

columns.name: Object Name
columns.description: The type of expense being paid. In the private sector, this would be called a general ledger account, however in the government sector, these are most often referred to as objects. Examples include Payroll, Professional Services or Travel.

columns.name: Sub Object Code
columns.description: The number assigned to line items within an object to track specific expenditures related to that object.

columns.name: Sub Object Name
columns.description: The name of the sub object.

columns.name: Amount
columns.description: The amount of the transaction

columns.name: Document ID
columns.description: The number assigned to the transaction by the accounting system.

columns.name: Date Valid
columns.description: The time and date the data was last updated from the accounting system.

columns.name: Year Valid
columns.description: Year derived from the “Date Valid” field.

columns.name: Vendor Customer Code
columns.description: Number assigned to a vendor for tracking purposes.

columns.name: Vendor Legal Name
columns.description: Plain Text   Legal name of the vendor paid

columns.name: Revenue Source Name
columns.description: The name of the revenue source.

name: Streetlight Fixtures

columns.name: Symbol Type
columns.description: This field is a concatenation of Usage, Fixture Type, Lamp Type, and Photocell. It is used to symbolize a feature on a map.

columns.name: Symbol Rotation
columns.description: This field indicates the degree of rotation of the symbol in a map.

columns.name: Conversion Month Date
columns.description: If available, the date representing the month that the streetlight lamp type was converted to LED.

columns.name: Fixture Number
columns.description: This field indicates the number assigned to the fixture.

columns.name: Object ID
columns.description: This field is a computer-generated unique ID.

columns.name: Shield
columns.description: This field indicates whether there is a shield on the fixture or not.

columns.name: Quarter Section
columns.description: This field indicates the quarter section containing the fixture.

columns.name: Link Key
columns.description: This field is a reference ID for an associated street segment.

columns.name: Small Cell
columns.description: This is the name of the company who has a cellular tower attached to pole, if applicable.

columns.name: Monitor System
columns.description: This field indicates the name of the monitor system company.

columns.name: Photo Cell
columns.description: This field indicates whether a photo cell is installed on the fixture or not. A photoconductive cell turns streetlights on and off automatically according to the level of daylight. One photo cell can control multiple streetlights.

columns.name: Distribution Type
columns.description: This field describes the type of shape the light takes on the ground.

columns.name: Color Correlated Temperature
columns.description: The Correlated Color Temperature (CCT) is a specification of the color appearance of the light emitted by a lamp measured in degrees Kelvin (K).

columns.name: Location
columns.description: Streetlight Location

columns.name: Usage
columns.description: This field designates what the lighting is for: Roadway, Novelty (decorative), or Pedestrian.

columns.name: Fixture Type
columns.description: This field indicates the type of fixture: Cobra Head, Column, Decorative, Post Top, Shoebox, Transit, Wall Pack.

columns.name: Brand
columns.description: This field indicates the brand of the fixture: American Electric, Cooper, Cree, Firstlight, GE, Kim, Phillips, Sternberg, Sterner, Wild West.

columns.name: Model Description
columns.description: This field describes the model of the fixture.

columns.name: Path Pole ID
columns.description: This field indicates the unique identifier for the pole, if applicable.

columns.name: Path ID
columns.description: This field indicates the name of the Multi-use Pathway that the fixture is on, if applicable.

columns.name: Letter of Acceptance Month
columns.description: The Month of the Letter of Acceptance: this is the date a project passes final inspection.

columns.name: Letter of Acceptance Year
columns.description: The Year of the Letter of Acceptance: this is the date a project passes final inspection.

columns.name: Letter of Acceptance
columns.description: This field indicates the name of the project that the streetlight installation corresponds to.

columns.name: Project Name
columns.description: This field indicates the name of the project that the streetlight installation corresponds to.

columns.name: Cabinet Number
columns.description: This is the number of the cabinet that the fixture is assigned to.

columns.name: Cabinet ID
columns.description: This number correlates to the Link ID in the Cabinet Feature Class.

columns.name: Facility ID
columns.description: This field is a computer-generated unique identifier used for Cityworks, which is our work management system.

columns.name: Lumens
columns.description: This field indicates the lumens of the lamp, or the amount of light a certain lamp gives off.

columns.name: Boundary
columns.description: The field indicates which City of Mesa Streetlights Crew is responsible for the fixture: East or West.

columns.name: Model Number
columns.description: This field indicates the model number of the fixture.

columns.name: Lamp Type
columns.description: This field describes the type of lamp used in the fixture: high pressure sodium (HPS), light-emitting diode (LED), or metal halide (MH).

columns.name: Wattage
columns.description: This field indicates the wattage of the lamp, or the amount of energy it takes to produce a certain amount of light.

columns.name: Pole
columns.description: This field describes the type of pole used for the fixture.

columns.name: County Light
columns.description: This field indicates whether the fixture is within Maricopa County jurisdiction or not.

columns.name: Cityworks Work Order
columns.description: This field indicates the Cityworks Work Order number. Cityworks is our work management system.

columns.name: Verified
columns.description: This field indicates whether the fixture is staff verified or not.

columns.name: Utility
columns.description: This field indicates who provides the electrical feed for the fixture: Salt River Project (SRP) or City Of Mesa (COM).

columns.name: Feed Method
columns.description: This field indicates the type of power feed to the fixture: Cabinet, Individual, Metered Pedestal, or Unmetered Pedestal.

description: This feature class identifies all streetlight fixtures within the boundary of the City of Mesa, including portions of other bordering agencies. A streetlight fixture includes the lamp, pole, and photocell, if present. Data are stored as a point geometry type feature class in the City Enterprise Geodatabase. The projected coordinate system is NAD 1983 State Plane Arizona Central FIPS 0202 Feet.

description: Monthly electric consumption data in kWh and customer counts within the City of Mesa's electric service territory broken down by Rate Classification (i.e. Residential, Commercial, Other Public Authority, Interdepartmental). Consumption totals are for the month in which they are billed to the customer (“Billing Month”). Due to the constant reading of utility meters throughout the month, much of the consumption occurs in the month prior to the Billing Month. Data reporting may have a 14 day lag.

tags: average frequency of interruptions per customer
description: This dataset allows for the monthly computation of the System Average Interruption Frequency Index (SAIFI) for the City of Mesa’s electric utility. The System Average Interruption Frequency Index is commonly used as a reliability indicator by electric power utilities. It measures the average outage frequency per customer and is calculated by taking the sum of the total customers interrupted divided by the total number of active customers. Data reporting may have a 14 day lag.

name: Transportation Pavement Inventory

columns.name: Object ID
columns.description: This field is a computer-generated unique ID.

The Link Key is classified by street type: arterial, collector, dirt road, intersection, parking lot, miscellaneous, other, paved alley, residential, unpaved alley, retired.

columns.name: Facility ID
columns.description: This field is a computer-generated unique identifier used for Cityworks, which is our work management system.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: This field identifies the name and boundaries of the section of roadway or paved area.

columns.description: This field describes the type of intersection: Decorative Full, Decorative Partial, Painted Bike Facility.

columns.name: Condition Year

description: This feature class identifies all roadways and parking areas that are maintained by the City of Mesa. This includes streets, intersections, parking lots, paved alleys, unpaved alleys, and dirt roads. Data are stored as a line geometry type feature class in the City Enterprise Geodatabase. The projected coordinate system is NAD 1983 State Plane Arizona Central FIPS 0202 Feet.

name: CIP Budget

columns.name: Major Program Category Name
columns.description: The lowest level of classification of City projects.  The projects assigned to each major program class are further separated into smaller groupings.  Similar to the major program class, all City projects are only assigned to one of these classifications.

columns.name: Major Program Name
columns.description: A project the City is working on. Generally, a major program is created to capture all of the costs of creating a new or improving an existing asset before the asset is placed in service. Major programs may also track costs related to a grant awarded to the City.

columns.name: Phase Code
columns.description: The number assigned to a phase of a project.

columns.name: Phase Name
columns.description: A project is split into phases which generally represent Pre-design/Design/Construction or may be split into Purchases (where no construction is involved in the acquisition of the asset).

columns.name: Project Budget
columns.description: The current project budget for the total project cost over the life of the project.

columns.name: Fund Name
columns.description: The governmental segment that benefited from the transaction. For the capital dataset, we have set a default name for this as Capital Project Funding as funds are not loaded in Budget Structure 37.

columns.name: Major Program Class Name
columns.description: The highest level of classification of City projects.  There are currently five different classifications; General Government, Parks & Culture, Public Safety, Transportation, and Utilities. All projects are assigned to one of these classifications.

columns.name: Major Program Category
columns.description: The number assigned to the major program category.

columns.name: Program Name
columns.description: A program represents a smaller segment of a project the City is working on. For example, the project is installing a full irrigation system and each program will represent each park included in the project.

columns.name: Major Program Class
columns.description: The number assigned to the major program classification.

columns.name: Project Actuals
columns.description: The current accumulated expenditures for a specific project.

columns.name: Program Code
columns.description: The number assigned to a program.

columns.name: Fiscal Year
columns.description: A 12-month period of time to which the Annual Budget applies and at the end of which, a governmental unit determines its financial position and the results of its operations.  For the City of Mesa, the fiscal year is July 1 through June 30.

columns.name: Major Program
columns.description: The number assigned to a project.

description: Listing of all financially active City project budgets and expenses from FIN’s budget structure 37 data. The dataset will be pulled once a month for Socrata, and the dataset will represent a point in time as the budget structure will be a live data set. columns.description: Indicator used to tally “transferred to external” and “warm transfer” calls

columns.description: Indicator that call was abandoned by the caller

columns.description: Number of seconds caller stayed in service before the call disconnecting

columns.description: The abandoned call rate is calculated by dividing the number of calls flagged as abandoned by the total number of all calls per month.

columns.name: Year
columns.description: Year of activity

columns.description: Indicator used to tally “answered” calls.  Additional field

columns.description: Indicator if call was handled within the time frame and goals set for the service

columns.name: Call Offered
columns.description: Indicator that the call was offered to a logged in user

description: This dataset provides information regarding the number of telephone calls that are handled by the Court’s IVR (Interactive Voice Response) system and the Court’s Resources (Agents). Includes automatic Call Distribution Inbound Services (queue) Call Detail Data. Information includes call totals, duration and service level statistics. Information in this dataset is used for calculation of Court performance measures. See Municipal Court - Telephone IVR Activity at https://citydata.mesaaz.gov/datase

description: NOTE: Effective March 1, 2019, the Mesa tax rate increased from 1.75% to 2.0%. Period revenues are delayed by up to 120 days. For example, May revenues are generally posted by mid July. This dataset contains sales tax revenue for taxpayers within downtown Mesa by posting month. Posting month is the accounting period the City booked the revenues and is used for financial reporting and budgeting purposes. To see a map of downtown Mesa boundaries see the "Source Link" in the 'About this Dataset' se

description: Information about the the timeliness of datasets published on the portal as measured by age of most recent data in each dataset compared with Max Expected Data Age. A second FME workflow sends that currency data to this Socrata dataset. To remove a dataset from this check, enter 0 as the Max Expected Data Age metadata field

description: Historical information about the projects currently in process and managed by the Department of Information Technology. Projects are noted if they are in an implementation phase, which would make their schedule and budget adherence applicable. Budget is listed and determined by project manager if they are within budget, at or under budget, to date. Schedule is determined by project start date, project manager original go live estimate and current go live estimate and /or actual go live date.

description: This dataset describes planned and completed traffic signal audits across the City of Mesa managed transportation system. A traffic signal audit is a formal examination of the number of signalized intersections maintained by the City of Mesa. They are checked for proper programming and operation on a quarterly basis by a formal internal team. The data recorded includes the date by which each intersection needed to be audited, as well as the date the audit was completed.

description: All HR Training instructor lead classes (in classroom and virtual) are included in this dataset. Employees evaluate the training on a 1 (Strongly Disagree) - 5 (Strongly Agree) scale related to how the training overall met their learning need. HR Training staff review the course evaluations completed post class for our instructor lead classes and take the average the Overall rating each the month for all of these classes. The metric is based on the percentage of 4-(Agree) and 5 -(Strongly Agree)

description: This dataset allows for the monthly computation of the System Average Interruption Duration Index (SAIDI) for the City of Mesa’s electric utility. The System Average Interruption Duration Index is commonly used as a reliability indicator by electric power utilities. It measures the average outage duration for each customer served and is calculated by taking the sum of the total customer minutes of service interruptions and dividing that by the total number of customers served. columns.description: This is a unique identifier for each record in the dataset.

columns.description: Sourced fromTotal number of damages in the Mesa and Magma Service Areas. A “damage” would be anytime an excavator unintentionally or intentionally hits an underground utility while excavating.  the Damage Prevention dataset - see dataset description

columns.name: Costs Magma S180
columns.description: Sum of the locate costs for the current month for the Magma Service Area. Costs may include, Wages, benefits, marking paint to complete locates, locating equipment repair, tools, Fees for 811 membership, etc.

columns.name: Month
columns.description: The accounting period in a Fiscal year in which the 811 locate occurred, based on 12 accounting periods.

columns.name: Total Hits per 1000 Locates Any Reason
columns.description: Total damages for month multiplied by 1000 then divided total number of locates for current month.

columns.description: Total locating costs in the Magma Service Area divided by the number of utility locates requested by excavators in the Magma Service Area.

columns.name: Average Cost Per Mesa Locate
columns.description: Total locating costs in the Mesa Service Area divided by the number of utility locates requested by excavators in the Mesa Service Area.

columns.description: Total number of locates requested by an excavator or homeowner in the Mesa Service Area only. https://www.mesaaz.gov/residents/energy/service-area-maps

columns.name: Total Hits Per 1000 Locates
columns.description: Total damages for month (if Mesa at fault) multiplied by 1000 then divided total number of locates for current month. Used to establish a baseline widely used in the locate industry.

columns.name: 12 Month Rolling Average
columns.description: 12-month rolling average of the average cost per locate in both the Magma and Mesa Service Areas.

columns.name: Total Locates
columns.description: Total number of locates in the Mesa and Magma Service Areas. A “Locate” would be considered any response by City of Mesa personnel, either electronic, painting the location of underground utilities or speaking with the excavator.

columns.description: Total number of locates requested by an excavator or homeowner in the Magma Service Area only. https://www.mesaaz.gov/residents/energy/service-area-maps

columns.name: Costs Mesa S179
columns.description: Sum of the locate costs for current month for the Mesa Service Area. Costs may include, Wages, benefits, marking paint to complete locates, locating equipment repair, tools, Fees for 811 membership, etc.

columns.name: Date
columns.description: The day of the month based on the accounting period and calendar year on which the 811 locate occurred.

columns.name: Year
columns.description: Fiscal Year that the 811 locate was responded to by City of Mesa locate personnel.

description: This dataset contains the necessary information to compute the average cost of a utility locate in the Mesa and/or Magma Service Areas (see Service Area maps). A utility locate involves identification of all underground utilities prior to excavation in a specified area. columns.name: Business Objective
columns.description: The number assigned to a major line of business that aligns with one or more of the Council's strategic initiatives; denotes a primary public purpose; and defines where the City allocates its resources.

columns.description: The type of revenue or expense being paid. In the private sector, this would be called a general ledger account, however in the government sector, these are most often referred to as objects. Examples include Payroll, Professional Services or Travel.

columns.name: Fiscal Year
columns.description: A 12-month period of time to which the Annual Budget applies and at the end of which, a governmental unit determines its financial position and the results of its operations. For the City of Mesa, the fiscal year is July 1 through June 30.

columns.name: Appropriation Name
columns.description: The type of appropriation of the transaction. These will either be the base operating budget or a capital improvement project.

columns.name: Adopted Budget

columns.name: Service Level
columns.description: The number assigned to a group of core business processes related by a common purpose (mission, outcomes, and expected performance). This level of structure is optional depending on departmental needs.

columns.name: Business Objective Name
columns.description: A major line of business that aligns with one or more of the Council's strategic initiatives; denotes a primary public purpose; and defines where the City allocates its resources. Examples include Fleet Services and Economic Development.

name: Business Services - Credit Services Calls

columns.name: Call Handled
columns.description: A call is counted as handled when the call is answered by an agent.

columns.name: Call Abandoned by System
columns.description: A call is counted as abandoned when the caller is routed to other options.

columns.name: Call Abandoned
columns.description: A call is counted as abandoned when the caller hangs up before the call is answered by an agent or before the call is routed to other options.

columns.name: Call Type ID
columns.description: This identifies the type of call ID # that comes into the ASPECT system. Examples: 75, 84, 77, etc. (This is what the Customer is calling for)

columns.name: Call Type Description
columns.description: This data describes the type of call. For example, the different types of calls are as follows: Continuance, Court Dates, General Information, Make Payments, Photo Safety, Bonds, etc.

columns.name: Service ID
columns.description: The numeric ID assigned to a service.

columns.name: Service
columns.description: A service is a grouping of calls based on the needs of the customer and the prompts they select during the call.

columns.name: Application ID
columns.description: The numeric ID assigned to an Application.

columns.name: Application Description
columns.description: The application is the topmost grouping of all services., For example Application: CIS_CustomerSvc includes all services for both the Customer Service and Credit call centers.

columns.name: Date
columns.description: Date

columns.name: Spanish Flag
columns.description: Marked if the service is designated for Spanish callers.

columns.name: Row ID
columns.description: Unique Row Identifier

columns.name: Call Abandoned by Customer
columns.description: A call is counted as abandoned by customer when the caller hangs up before the call is answered.

columns.name: Year
columns.description: Year

columns.name: Month
columns.description: Month

columns.name: Wrap Duration
columns.description: The amount of time an agent spends in wrap status after a call.

columns.name: Incoming Call
columns.description: A call that has been placed into the city ACD system.

columns.name: Handled To Agent
columns.description: Indicator that the call was transferred to agent.

columns.name: Total Duration
columns.description: Number  Total duration of the call in number of seconds. Includes hold, talk, and wrap times

columns.name: Calls Served
columns.description: A value of 1 means the call offered was not abandoned (Call Abandoned) or “answered” (Handled Completed). A value of 0 means that the call offered was abandoned (Call Abandoned) or “answered” (Handled Completed).

columns.name: Display Description
columns.description: Description of disposition if used otherwise “unknown” as not having a disposition.

columns.name: Handled Within Service Level
columns.description: Indicator if call was handled within the time frame and goals set for the service.

columns.name: Handled To Service
columns.description: ndicator that the call was handed by a service.

columns.name: Handled Other
columns.description: Indicator used to tally “transferred to external” and “warm transfer” calls.

columns.name: Handled Completed
columns.description: Indicator used to tally “answered” calls. Additional field.

columns.name: End Date Time
columns.description: The date the call ended.

columns.name: Begin Date Time
columns.description: The date and time a call started.

columns.name: Begin Date
columns.description: The date the call started.

columns.name: Talk Duration
columns.description: The amount of time a caller speaks with an agent.

columns.name: Hold Duration
columns.description: The amount of time a call is held in queue.

columns.name: Answer Duration
columns.description: The amount of time prior to the call being answered.

columns.name: Abandon Duration
columns.description: The amount of time prior to the call being abandoned.

columns.name: Short Call
columns.description: A call that falls under the given threshold for that service. I.E. many CustomerService short call thresholds are set to 10 seconds. If the caller hangs up before that time the call is not included in statistics.

columns.name: Call Offered
columns.description: Number of calls offered during the time period.

description: This dataset contains information about phone calls to Credit Services. The data includes hold times, call times and if the call was answered or abandoned.

description: In the City of Mesa, RideChoice provides taxi service to those certified as ADA Paratransit eligible at a deeply discounted rate. Service is available 24 hours a day, 365 days a year, and you can choose your own taxi provider. There is no requirement to book your trip in advance, and you do not have to share your ride. The amount and cost of service varies by community of residence, but in most cases, shorter trips cost less than or equal to a comparable ADA Paratransit trip

name: Human Resources - Experience Modification Rate

columns.name: Calendar Year
columns.description: The City’s E-MOD is reviewed annually and is based on data provided from previous calendar years.  The Calendar year spans from January 1st through December 31st.

columns.name: Calendar Year Date
columns.description: The Industrial Commission of Arizona determines the E-MOD rate and reports it to the City sometime between March and April following the end of the calendar year.

columns.name: Experience Modification
columns.description: The rate and measure of future financial risk based on current and historical losses (costs) related to workplace injuries.  The data reviewed includes current year plus 3 years prior experience.  The Industrial Commission of Arizona uses insurance industry reports of similar companies to analyze and compare the City's performance for the purpose of establishing the EMOD rate.

description: The Experience Modification (E-MOD) is an industry rating used by insurance companies for the calculation of workers’ compensation insurance premiums. The E-MOD rates and measures the future financial risk based on current and historical losses (costs) related to workplace injuries. The City administers a self-insured workers’ compensation program and is not required to pay workers’ compensation insurance; however, the City is required to contribute to state funds, in the form of taxes, used to

description: This dataset includes all statuses for status changes associated with Code Compliance cases. Code cases move through various workflow tasks and status prior to closing the case. For this reason, a distinct code case may be represented by multiple rows or records for each change in workflow and workflow status. name: Mesa High School Dropout Rate
description: Dropout rates reported by Mesa area high schools by cohort year sourced from https://www.azed.gov/accountability-research/data Dropout rates cover grades 7–12 and are measured from the first day of summer recess through the last day of the following school year. A dropout is a student who was enrolled at some point during the school year but did not finish — and did not transfer, graduate, or pass away. The dropout rate is calculated using each school's total enrollment.

name: High Pollution Advisory Days
